subject: [PATCH] mm/vmstat: spread vmstat_update requeue across the stat interval

reviews.author: Breno Leitao
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that vmstat_shepherd() also queues work simultaneously on all CPUs, potentially causing lock contention under sustained memory pressure, and suggested incorporating spreading logic in a v2 of the patch or as a separate follow-up patch.
reviews.raw_body:
Good point - incorporating similar spreading logic in vmstat_shepherd()
would indeed address the simultaneous queueing issue you've described.

Should I include this in a v2 of this patch, or would you prefer it as
a separate follow-up patch?

reviews.author: Usama Arif (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged the potential issue of lock contention in vmstat_shepherd() and suggested it could be addressed in a separate follow-up patch, but did not commit to revising this specific patch.
reviews.raw_body:
I think it can be a separate follow-up patch, but no strong preference.
For this patch:

Acked-by: Usama Arif <usama.arif@linux.dev>

reviews.author: Breno Leitao
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that vmstat_shepherd() queues work with delay 0 on all CPUs, which could re-trigger lock contention under sustained memory pressure when the shepherd fires every sysctl_stat_interval.
reviews.raw_body:
Thanks!

I will send a follow-up patch soon.
--breno

2026-04-01.patch_summary: This patch modifies vmstat_update to spread its requeue across the stat interval, reducing lock contention on large systems under sustained memory pressure.
